history.text: The Kingdom of Rwanda emerged, giving the area a defined monarchical state structure.
The territory became part of German East Africa, shaping its later political and regional identity.
The territory became part of Ruanda-Urundi, shaping its later political and regional identity.
The Rwandan Revolution reshaped Rwanda's political life.
A republic was declared, changing Rwanda's political system and state identity.
Rwanda became independent from Belgium, marking a key step in modern statehood.
Rwanda was admitted to the United Nations, confirming broad international recognition.
The current constitution came into force, setting the basic framework for today's Rwanda.
